changes: Get-TenantLicenseReport.ps1: now reads columns A (Domain) and C/D (App ID / App Secret) from the tenant workbook alongside tenant ID and skip flag

Tenants with a stored App ID + App Secret connect automatically via client-credentials (app-only) auth — no sign-in at all — falling back to interactive if that fails (e.g. the app registration lacks Organization.Read.All)
Tenants without stored app credentials show a prominent '>>> SIGN IN TO: <domain> <<<' banner in the log right before the browser opens, so it's always clear which tenant/account to sign in with
Report CSV now includes the tenant's Domain column

Misc Scripts: added 'Tenant License Report' — reads tenant IDs from the standing Volaris tenant workbook (column B, skipping rows with 'Yes' in column L) and connects to each in turn, reporting only licenses that grant a mailbox and/or OneDrive (excludes add-on-only SKUs like EMS, Entra ID P1/P2, Power BI, Defender, Visio, Project, Teams Phone), writing one consolidated CSV

New Get-TenantLicenseReport.ps1: reads .xlsx (ImportExcel, positional column-letter access) or .csv (auto-detected header column), reuses search-domain.ps1's SKU friendly-name lookup table, uses the pinned-module Graph connect pattern (no device code, no WAM mandate issue). -TenantsFile/-TenantIdColumn/-SkipColumn/-SkipValue all overridable via parameters

Rolled the device-code fix out to every script that connects to Graph/Exchange Online headlessly (spawned by the app with no visible window) — confirmed live failures today on Update-SIPDomain.ps1 ('A window handle must be configured') and Retire-Devices.ps1 (same error) showed the problem wasn't limited to Discovery: WAM hard-requires a window that a headless spawn simply doesn't have, and its device-code fallback is now blocked tenant-wide by a Conditional Access policy

New Ensure-GraphModules.ps1 — shared helper (dot-sourced) that pins whichever Microsoft.Graph.* submodules a script uses to 2.33.0 (last version before the SDK made WAM mandatory), installing side-by-side if missing. Replaces duplicated ad-hoc module-check blocks in every script below

Graph: removed -UseDeviceCode/-UseDeviceAuthentication and added the pinned-module helper to Add-ExchangeAdmin.ps1, Check-OneDriveStatus.ps1, Connect-MicrosoftGraph.ps1, New-AzureAppRegistration.ps1, Provision-OneDrives.ps1, Remove-EntraUsers.ps1, Remove-devices.ps1, Retire-Devices.ps1, Set-TeamsOwners-Run.ps1, Update-SIPDomain.ps1, Update-UPN.ps1 (all 3 connect sites, including the visible-window device-code helper script it spawns for its WinForms Connect button)

Exchange Online: added -DisableWAM (documented fix for 'A window handle must be configured') to every bare Connect-ExchangeOnline call — Hide-AddressBook.ps1 (x2), Remove-AliasAddresses.ps1, Rename-DomainObjects.ps1, remove-domain.ps1 (x2), search-domain.ps1's two mid-run parallel-runspace reconnects, Update-SIPDomain.ps1

Fixed an unrelated pre-existing bug found along the way: Rename-DomainObjects.ps1 and remove-domain.ps1 both had "$label:" inside a double-quoted string (PowerShell parses this as an invalid scope-qualified variable reference) — this was a genuine parse error that would have made both scripts fail to run at all past that point. Fixed to "${label}:" in both

Domain Removal Workflow: added 'Clear Employee ID (On-Prem)' as step 4, right after 'Hide from Address Book' — steps renumbered 1-9 and the Next-button chain updated (Hide → Clear Employee ID → Remove Aliases) so the guided flow includes it

Moved 'Clear Employee ID (On-Prem)' from Misc Scripts to Domain Removal, positioned right after 'Hide from Address Book' — it's an on-prem AD cleanup step used during domain removal, not a general-purpose misc tool
View renamed from miscClearEmployeeIdView to domainClearEmployeeIdView and gained the '← Workflow' back button to match its Domain Removal siblings; no changes to Clear-EmployeeId.ps1 itself

Misc Scripts: added 'Clear Employee ID (On-Prem)' — reads the Source identity column from an AvePoint mapping CSV and clears the EmployeeID attribute for each matching user in on-premise Active Directory

New Clear-EmployeeId.ps1: auto-detects the Source column (same convention as Import-FlyMappings.ps1), looks users up by UserPrincipalName or mail, WhatIf preview checked by default. Requires the ActiveDirectory RSAT module — this is an on-prem AD operation, not Entra ID/cloud

search-domain.ps1 (v2.12.2): the SharePoint and Power Platform sections were silently skipping themselves whenever their module (Microsoft.Online.SharePoint.PowerShell / Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.PowerShell) wasn't already installed on the machine, instead of installing it

Ensure-Module now actually auto-installs missing modules from PSGallery (CurrentUser scope) rather than just checking for them and telling you to install manually — applies to all four SPO/Power-Platform module checks (upfront scan + mid-run fallback, for each)

CORRECTION to v2.9.15: confirmed live against celcat.com that Set-MgGraphOption -DisableLoginByWAM does NOT actually stop WAM being used — it's a documented Microsoft SDK bug/limitation (only works if paired with a custom app registration, which this toolkit doesn't have)

search-domain.ps1 (v2.12.1), Invoke-TenantBaseline.ps1: now pin Microsoft.Graph.Authentication (and, for the baseline script, Groups/DeviceManagement/DeviceManagement.Enrollment) to 2.33.0 — the last SDK version before WAM became mandatory in 2.34.0 — installed side-by-side, doesn't touch any newer version already on a machine

This actually restores plain browser-popup interactive sign-in with no new app registration or per-tenant admin consent needed; device-code fallback paths are kept as a last resort but should no longer be needed

search-domain.ps1 (v2.12.0), Invoke-TenantBaseline.ps1: interactive Graph/Exchange Online sign-in now disables the WAM broker upfront (Set-MgGraphOption -DisableLoginByWAM / Connect-ExchangeOnline -DisableWAM) instead of relying on WAM to work and falling back to device code when it doesn't

Root cause: some tenants now block device-code flow tenant-wide via a Conditional Access 'Authentication flows' policy, so the long-standing WAM-fails-fall-back-to-device-code pattern in this codebase no longer has a working fallback on those tenants — avoiding WAM in the first place (browser popup instead) sidesteps both the WAM breakage and the device-code block

Device-code fallback paths are kept for older module versions that predate -DisableLoginByWAM/-DisableWAM, but now surface a clear 'blocked by tenant Conditional Access policy' message instead of a raw AADSTS error
This entry's fix turned out not to work — see v2.9.16

Invoke-TenantBaseline.ps1: Step 11's 'Block Access Outside Approved Countries' Conditional Access policy now trusts Ireland (IE) in addition to CA/US/GB — UK ISP traffic can geolocate to Dublin at the Microsoft/Azure network edge, which was blocking genuine GB sign-ins with AADSTS53003
If a tenant was already baselined before this fix, add a Trusted Country - IE named location and exclude it on that policy manually via the Entra admin portal (Conditional Access > Named locations)
Misc Scripts: added 'Tenant Baseline Config' — runs the full Entra ID/Intune/Exchange baseline pass in-app with a streaming log panel, same as the other Misc Scripts tools

New Invoke-TenantBaseline.ps1 (copy of the Jolera all-in-one baseline script): always invoked with -Restarted so it never kills other pwsh/powershell sessions or relaunches itself in a new window when run from the app; admin UPN is collected from the form instead of a Read-Host prompt
Invoke-TenantBaseline.ps1 writes its run log and Exchange audit log to %APPDATA%\FlyMigration\Logs instead of the script folder (read-only in a perMachine install)

Fix discovery-menu.ps1: child pwsh process now launches with a Normal window instead of Minimized — a minimized window has no HWND for the WAM broker to parent its sign-in dialog to, which silently forced Connect-MgGraph to fall back to device-code auth on every discovery run

Root cause: device-code auth is now blocked outright (AADSTS53003) on tenants whose Conditional Access baseline denies the Device Code Flow authentication transfer method, so discovery could no longer sign in on newly-baselined tenants
New Connect-MicrosoftGraph.ps1: authenticates to Graph once via device code with Sites.ReadWrite.All and User.Read.All scopes; shows existing session if already connected

New Provision-OneDrive-User.ps1: provisions OneDrive for one or more UPNs passed as parameters; reuses the existing Graph session without re-authenticating — run Connect-MicrosoftGraph.ps1 once, then call this script as many times as needed
discovery-menu.ps1: added SharePoint Admin URL field — enter the destination tenant's admin URL (https://tenant-admin.sharepoint.com) to override the saved config; leave blank to use the saved value
search-domain.ps1: added -SharePointAdminUrl parameter; takes priority over shared-config.json and the hardcoded default
New Rename-DomainObjects.ps1: clean standalone script containing only the rename logic — no WinForms GUI, no delete commands
App now calls Rename-DomainObjects.ps1 instead of remove-domain.ps1 for the in-app Rename Domain Objects view
remove-domain.ps1 retained as-is for the legacy standalone GUI window (launched without -DiscoveryFolder)

Fix Provision-OneDrives.ps1: replaced SPO module (Connect-SPOService + Request-SPOPersonalSite) with Microsoft Graph; authenticates via device code (code appears in log panel); triggers provisioning by calling Get-MgUserDrive per user — no browser popup required
Fix Check-OneDriveStatus.ps1: same Graph migration; checks drive existence via Get-MgUserDrive per user instead of Get-SPOSite bulk query

Fix New-SharePointSites.ps1, Remove-DeletedSharePointSites.ps1, Deduplicate-Inventory.ps1: now prefer PnP.PowerShell with -DeviceLogin (device code appears in log, no popup); fall back to SPO module if PnP not installed
Root cause: -UseWebLogin removed in SPO module v16.0.27313.12000; default Connect-SPOService MSAL browser popup is invisible when spawned as Electron child process

Fix SharePoint Online sign-in for all in-app scripts: added -UseWebLogin to Connect-SPOService in Provision-OneDrives.ps1, Check-OneDriveStatus.ps1, New-SharePointSites.ps1, Remove-DeletedSharePointSites.ps1, Deduplicate-Inventory.ps1 — the interactive popup dialog does not appear when spawned from Electron; -UseWebLogin opens the OS default browser instead, which works correctly
App Registration: after creating the app, Notepad opens automatically with Tenant ID, App ID, and Client Secret — file saved to Desktop as AppReg_<tenant>_<id>.txt
App Registration: credentials section now uses Write-Output (guaranteed stdout) so the secret is visible in the log; credential block clearly separated with markers
Added open-file IPC handler in main.js + preload.js; PS scripts can signal ##OPEN_FILE:<path>## to open any file via Electron shell.openPath instead of spawning from a child process
AOS Setup: added Step 1 — Assign Exchange Administrator role; enter the consent account UPN and click Assign Role before anything else; required for the Fly Exchange consent to succeed

New Add-ExchangeAdmin.ps1: connects to Microsoft Graph, looks up Exchange Administrator role definition, checks for existing assignment, and permanently assigns the role to the specified account; idempotent (no error if already assigned)
AOS Setup view restructured as 3 numbered steps: (1) Assign Exchange Admin, (2) Sign in to AOS, (3) Create App Profile and Grant Consent

Domain Removal workflow step 7: replaced 'Remove Devices' with 'Retire Devices' — disables devices in Entra ID (AccountEnabled = false) rather than permanently deleting them; device records are preserved
New Retire-Devices.ps1: reads 11_Devices.csv, disables each device via Update-MgDevice, same CSV format and Graph modules as Remove-devices.ps1
Misc Scripts: added 'Remove Entra Devices' (permanent delete) — moved from Domain Removal section; now a separate tool for when full deletion is required

Rename Domain Objects: completely rewritten — no longer deletes M365 objects; renames email domain on mailboxes, shared mailboxes, distribution groups, mail contacts, M365 groups/Teams, and proxy addresses using Set-Mailbox/Set-DistributionGroup/Set-UnifiedGroup/Set-MailContact
Rename Domain Objects: only the Accepted Domain entry is removed from the tenant (domain registration); all M365 objects are preserved

UI: view renamed from 'Remove Domain' to 'Rename Domain Objects'; added Old Domain and New Domain fields; removed App Registrations, Enterprise Apps, Devices from checkboxes; button changed from red to blue
Workflow step 8: relabelled 'Rename Domain Objects + Remove from Tenant'
Domain Removal Workflow: added Remove Devices as step 7 (after AD sync, before Remove Domain)

Fix Remove Devices, Remove Entra Users, Update SIP Domain (Remove mode): now explicitly imports Microsoft.Graph modules before connecting — fixes 'Connect-MgGraph is not recognized' error when Graph module not auto-loaded

Domain Removal: added 'Remove Devices' in-app tool — reads 11_Devices.csv from a discovery folder (or a specific CSV file) and removes devices from Entra ID via Microsoft Graph; WhatIf support; accepts DeviceObjectId or DeviceId column

Domain Removal: added 'Remove Entra Users' last-resort tool — exports full backup CSV (UPN, display name, licences, group memberships, proxy addresses, etc.) then deletes cloud-only users blocking domain removal; on-prem synced users skipped automatically
Domain Removal Workflow: last-resort callout box links directly to Remove Entra Users
Domain Removal: each step view now has '← Workflow' and 'Next: …→' navigation buttons to move between steps or return to the workflow overview

Remove Domain: now runs in-app — browse discovery folder, tick object types (Mailboxes, Groups, Contacts, Apps, Devices, Proxy Addresses, etc.), WhatIf preview, streaming output; no separate PowerShell window
remove-domain.ps1: added headless mode (-DiscoveryFolder, -Sections, -WhatIf params) used by the in-app form

Domain Removal Workflow: redesigned as a 7-step guide with 'Go →' buttons to navigate directly to each tool (Update On-Prem UPNs, Update Cloud UPNs, Hide from Address Book, Remove Aliases, SIP / IM Addresses, Entra Sync note, Remove Domain)
SIP / IM Addresses: Old Domain and New Domain fields are now blank by default — enter any domain, not just tarantosystems.com

SIP / IM Addresses: new Remove mode — assigns a temporary Exchange/Teams licence, waits for provisioning, deletes the SIP/EUM addresses, then revokes the licence; licence SKU auto-detected from tenant or can be specified manually
SIP / IM Addresses: mode selector in UI (Remove / Replace); Remove mode shows licence SKU and wait-time fields; Replace mode shows new-domain field
Sidebar label updated to 'SIP / IM Addresses'
Domain Removal: Added 'Update SIP Domain' in-app view — enter old and new domain, replaces SIP/EUM address domains across all EXO recipients; WhatIf checked by default for safety
Fix Remove-AliasAddresses.ps1: replaced [bool] params with [switch] (-SkipAliases, -SkipSIP) to fix 'Cannot convert System.String to Boolean' error when called from the app
Added Update-SIPDomain.ps1 — replaces the domain on SIP/EUM addresses across all EXO recipients (default: tarantosystems.com → ourvolaris.onmicrosoft.com); supports -WhatIf
Remove Aliases: added separate checkboxes to control what is removed — 'Alias SMTP addresses (smtp:)' and 'IM/SIP addresses (SIP:, EUM:)' can now be run independently
Domain Removal: Update On-Prem UPNs now runs in-app — browse CSV folder, enter source/target domain, stream output directly (no separate PowerShell window)
Domain Removal: Update Cloud UPNs now runs in-app — enter old/new domain, device-code sign-in appears in the log panel
Domain Removal: Hide from Address Book now runs in-app — browse discovery folder, stream EXO progress in-app
Domain Removal: Added 'Remove Aliases' tool — strips secondary smtp: and IM (SIP/EUM) addresses from recipients in discovery CSVs, optional domain filter
All three converted scripts support WhatIf mode for safe preview before making changes
Removed debug console logging from renderer and index.html
Discovery: domain combobox now works as plain text when no VBU CSV is configured — no longer blocks typing
Discovery: arrow keys (Up/Down) navigate the domain list; Enter selects; Escape closes
Check-Updates: auto-installs without asking — shows what's new and proceeds immediately
Settings: Check for Updates now streams output in real time so you can see each step
Settings: fixed Update Available banner never showing (UPDATE_AVAILABLE signal was missing from script)
Check-Updates: added 20-second timeout on GitHub version check — fails clearly instead of hanging
Import-FlyMappings: DNS pre-check logs whether API hostname resolves before connecting
Import-FlyMappings: BOM-aware CSV encoding detection (handles UTF-8, UTF-16, ANSI)
Import-FlyMappings: smarter source/destination column detection (prefers email/URL columns over plain name columns)
Added Check-OneDriveStatus.ps1, Get-UserMailboxLicenses.ps1, Set-DestinationLicenses.ps1
Added show-save-dialog IPC handler for native file save dialogs
Connections: added Stop Jobs button (red, right-aligned next to Clear) to stop in-progress Fly migration jobs
Added Stop-FlyMigrationStage.ps1 — calls Fly REST API DELETE to stop running jobs per workload
Discovery: default output folder changed to E:\Work\Jolera\Volaris\Discovery
Discovery: domain field replaced with searchable combobox — click ▾ or type to filter by domain or VBU name
Discovery: now runs search-domain.ps1 directly in the Electron UI (no separate window)
Discovery: domain list populated from VBU ID CSV; VBU ID auto-fills on selection
Discovery: Graph authentication falls back to device-code flow when WAM/HWND unavailable (fixes 0-users issue)
Discovery: output folder Browse now opens a folder picker; path is also editable
Discovery: -OutputPath parameter added to search-domain.ps1
Settings: added VBU CSV path field in Discovery tab
Fix: Save Settings no longer fails with JSON parse error on BOM-prefixed config.json (PS 5.1 compat)
Fix: settings.ps1 now writes config.json without UTF-8 BOM
Added Fix-ConfigBOM.ps1 — cleanup script for servers with existing BOM-prefixed config.json
Added Install-Prerequisites.bat + Install-Prerequisites.ps1 for one-step server setup
Added New-SharePointSites.ps1: creates missing destination SharePoint sites from a mapping CSV
Connections view: new 'Create Sites' button runs site creation before Verify (SharePoint only)
Connections view: added 'SharePoint site owner' email input for destination tenant admin UPN
Sites are created as modern team sites (STS#3 template) using Microsoft.Online.SharePoint.PowerShell
Fixed Import-FlyMappings: bypassed Fly.Client module's broken project lookup entirely
Root cause: Fly API search returns no results for names containing ' - ' (e.g. 'Pcentra - Exchange')
Fix: now searches with customer prefix only ('Pcentra'), finds project by exact name, posts mappings via REST API directly
Supports all workloads: Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, Teams, TeamChat, Groups
Added Deduplicate Inventory script to Misc Scripts (classifies Teams, Groups, SharePoint from discovery workbook)
Fixed Import-FlyMappings: removed unreliable project pre-check that blocked valid imports
Fixed Import-FlyMappings: NaN response from Fly API now correctly reported as a failure with diagnostics
Fixed Import-FlyMappings: CSV always normalised to UTF-8 BOM before passing to Fly.Client
Fixed Import-FlyMappings: source file encoding auto-detected from BOM (handles UTF-8, UTF-16, ANSI)
Fixed Import-FlyMappings: duplicate/same-row detection uses OrdinalIgnoreCase for non-ASCII characters
Fixed Import-FlyMappings: CSV column names and detected source/destination columns now logged
Fixed Start-FlyMigrationStage: removed hanging Get-FlyMigrationProject fallback scan
Fixed runner.ps1: removed invalid -Top parameter from Get-FlyMigrationProject call
Deduplicate Inventory: hardcoded SharePoint Admin URL to ourvolaris-admin.sharepoint.com
Log panels: added max-height cap so output scrolls instead of growing off-screen
Node.js: installed as portable (no admin required) with user PATH registration
Project monitor rows now open directly in Fly portal at the correct project URL
Fixed Fly portal URL construction to use /#/project/{id}/mappings format
Get-MigrationData: FailedItems and WarningItems now correctly populated from API
Get-MigrationData: ProjectId now included in workload summary for portal links
Start-FlyMigrationStage: skips workloads with no mappings imported
Start-FlyMigrationStage: handles already-in-progress mappings gracefully without error noise
Fixed open-logs button to open correct log directory (%APPDATA%\FlyMigration\Logs)
Fixed silent error in project monitor click handler (undeclared _cachedPortalUrl)
Improved error logging in Get-MigrationData (Write-Verbose -> Write-Warning)
Added Progress by Workload section with 4 workload cards
Shows progress for Exchange Online, OneDrive, SharePoint, and Teams
Visual progress bars with current/total counts
Added sidebar navigation to web edition
Web app now has Dashboard, Discovery, AvePoint Fly, Misc Scripts, Domain Removal quick links
Disabled DevTools auto-open in web app (Ctrl+Shift+I still works)
All menus now open maximized but remain resizable
Improved stat card labels - now show above values for clarity
Made stat labels uppercase and bold
Changed status text to muted color for better visual hierarchy
Stats now clearly indicate what each number represents
Redesigned main launcher as at-a-glance dashboard with live stats
Added 4 stat cards at top: Active Projects, Users Migrated, Success Rate, Total Data
Reorganized main menu tiles into 3-column grid layout
Enlarged main launcher to 1000x700px for better dashboard visibility
Added Quick Actions section header
Updated footer with version display
Moved dashboard stats from AvePoint Fly to main launcher
Redesigned AvePoint Fly menu as at-a-glance dashboard with live stats
Added 4 stat cards: Active Projects, Users Migrated, Success Rate, Total Data
Reorganized action cards into 3x2 grid layout
Added rounded corners to Discovery menu cards
Added smooth hover animations to all cards
Enlarged dashboard to 1000x700px for better visibility
Added Documentation quick action card
Refined UI to match official design system specifications
Standardized all colors to design system palette
Unified font definitions across all windows and dialogs
Improved dark grid styling in Project Monitor
Added complete color token library to lib.ps1
Rebranded from 'VG Migration Tools' to 'Migration Toolkit'
Updated all UI elements to use new branding
Updated documentation to reflect new product name
Added visual progress feedback during update installation
Improved auto-update visibility with notification banner
Update check now shows yellow banner when updates available
Added one-click 'Install Update' button to notification
Removed 'Update Cloud UPNs' from Domain Removal menu
Updated New-Package.ps1 to include all new domain removal files
Added version.json and Check-Updates.ps1 to package
Package now includes complete auto-update system
Added 'Check for Updates' button in Settings > Config tab
Removed 'Remove Devices' from Domain Removal menu
Added Domain-Removal-Workflow.ps1 - 3-step guided workflow
Added Update-OnPremUPN.ps1 for on-premise Active Directory
Fixed Hide-AddressBook.ps1 ExchangeOnlineManagement module errors
Added account preview list to Hide-AddressBook
Integrated Azure AD Sync functionality (VOL-ane-aad1)
Split Domain Removal menu: Workflow + individual tools
Hardcoded target domain to @ourvolaris.onmicrosoft.com
Added Update-UPN.ps1 with checkbox selection
Added Remove-devices.ps1 with DeviceObjectId support
Fixed column sorting in Update-UPN
Improved error handling across all scripts
Added comprehensive README documentation
Initial release
Main menu system
Discovery tools
Domain removal tools
AvePoint Fly integration
